summary:
Packed Red Blood Cell (RBC) transfusions ideally begin quickly post-receipt from storage given potential quality degradation over time; timely administration aligns closely with medical best practice recommendations prioritizing patient care efficacy without undue lag or interruption.
To address your request accurately while adhering strictly within two concise sentences:

Packed Red Blood Cells (pRBC) transfusions are recommended to start immediately after receiving them from the blood bank to maintain optimal cellular integrity. This approach supports rapid intervention based on clinical indications like symptomatic anemia, consistent across various professional guidelines aimed at enhancing treatment effectiveness. 

This summary captures the essence by highlighting immediate initiation benefits tied directly back to improving outcomes through prompt action aligned with established standards.
